levels.title: Quran basics
levels.blurb: Complete the verse. Every correct answer is the exact word in the text.

questions.q: Q 1:2 — '[All] praise is [due] to Allāh, Lord of the ___'
Q 2:255 (Ayat al-Kursi) — Allah is never overtaken by 'drowsiness or ___'
Q 2:256 — 'There shall be no ___ in [acceptance of] the religion'
Q 2:106 — when Allah substitutes a verse He brings one 'better than it or ___ to it'
Q 2:23 — 'then produce a ___ the like thereof' [if you doubt the Quran]
Q 4:3 — 'marry those that please you of [other] women, two or three or ___'
Q 4:34 — 'Men are ___ over women by [right of] what Allah has given one over the other'
Q 96:1 — the first verse of revelation begins: '___ in the name of your Lord who created'
Q 112:3 — 'He neither begets nor ___'
Q 18:86 — Dhul-Qarnayn followed the sun until he found it 'setting in a spring of ___'
Q 67:5 — the nearest heaven was adorned with 'lamps' and made them 'missiles against the ___'
Q 33:40 — Muhammad is 'the Messenger of Allāh and ___ of the prophets'

levels.title: Aisha & the household
levels.blurb: Exact words from the Quran and Bukhari on Muhammad's marriages.

questions.q: Bukhari — Aisha said the Prophet 'married her when she was a girl of ___ years of age'
Bukhari — the marriage was 'consummated' when Aisha was ___ years old
Q 33:37 — 'So when Zayd had no longer any need for her, We ___ her to you'
Q 33:4 — Allah 'has not made your claimed sons your [true] ___'
Q 33:6 — 'The Prophet is more worthy of the believers than their own ___'
Q 33:28 — wives who choose 'this world and its adornment' will receive a 'gracious ___'
Q 66:1 — 'O Prophet, why do you ___ [yourself from] what Allāh has made lawful for you'
Q 24:11 — the verse exonerating Aisha opens: 'Indeed, those who came with the ___'
Bukhari — Aisha said she used to play with ___ and the Prophet would join in
Q 33:53 — 'it would never be lawful for you to ___ his wives after him, ever'
Q 33:50 — uniquely grants the Prophet women 'whom his right hand ___'
Q 33:56 — 'Indeed, Allāh confers ___ upon the Prophet, and His angels [ask Him to do so]'

levels.title: Daily-life weirdness
levels.blurb: The exact prescriptions. Find the words in Bukhari and Muslim.

questions.q: Bukhari on the fly — 'in one of its wings there is a disease and in the other there is ___'
Bukhari — the Prophet instructed ill men to drink camel ___ as medicine alongside camel milk
Bukhari — Satan 'spends the night in the ___ part of your nose' if you don't rinse at dawn
Bukhari — the Prophet 'was bewitched so that he began to imagine that he had done a thing that he had ___'
Muslim — a ___ dog, a donkey, or a woman passing in front of a praying man cuts off his prayer
Bukhari — the rooster crows at night because 'it has seen an ___'
Bukhari — the Prophet had the physical strength of ___ men, enabling him to visit all his wives in one night
Muslim on the lizard (dabb) — the Prophet said: 'I do not ___ it, nor do I prohibit it'
Bukhari — the Prophet said grey hair should be changed but not to resemble the ___
Bukhari — when cleansing after the toilet with stones the number must be ___
Muslim — the Prophet said a person should drink water while ___, not while standing
Bukhari — the sunnah sleeping position is on the ___ side

levels.title: Cosmology & nature
levels.blurb: Heavens, earth, sun, embryo. Complete the verse exactly.

questions.q: Q 21:30 — 'the heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We ___ them'
Q 65:12 — 'It is Allāh who has created ___ heavens and of the earth, the like of them'
Q 78:7 — mountains are described as '___'
Q 86:6-7 — man was created from 'a fluid, ejected, emerging from between the ___ and the ribs'
Q 71:16 — Allāh made the sun 'a burning ___'
Bukhari — the sun goes each evening and '___ itself underneath the Throne' before rising
Q 22:5 — embryonic development passes through 'a clump of flesh, ___ and unformed'
Q 23:14 — from the clump, 'We made ___, and We covered the bones with flesh'
Q 79:30 — 'And after that He ___ the earth'
Q 25:54 — man was created 'from ___'
Q 38:71 — Allāh told the angels: 'I am going to create a human being from ___'
Q 96:2 — man was created 'from a ___ clot'

levels.title: Warfare & conquest
levels.blurb: Exact phrases on fighting, booty, and conquest. Find them in the Quran and Bukhari.

questions.q: Q 9:5 (Sword Verse) — 'kill the ___ wherever you find them'
Q 9:29 — fight them until they give 'the jizyah willingly while they are ___'
Q 8:41 — 'one ___ of it is for Allāh and for the Messenger' regarding war booty
Q 8:12 — Allāh said: 'I will cast ___ into the hearts of those who disbelieved'
Q 47:4 — '___ [their] necks until, when you have inflicted slaughter upon them, then secure their bonds'
Q 8:65 — 'one of you' who is patient in battle can overcome ___
Q 9:111 — 'Allāh has ___ from the believers their lives and their properties [in exchange] for that they will have Paradise'
Q 2:216 — 'fighting has been ___ upon you while it is hateful to you'
Q 5:33 — punishments for those who wage war on Allāh include being 'killed or ___'
Q 4:89 — hypocrites who refuse to fight: 'take them and ___ them wherever you find them'
Q 9:14 — 'Fight them; Allāh will ___ them by your hands'
Q 3:151 — 'We will cast ___ into the hearts of those who disbelieve'

levels.title: Hudud & punishment
levels.blurb: Precise numbers and exact phrases from the punishments. No paraphrasing.

questions.q: Q 24:2 — 'lash each one of them with ___ lashes' for the unmarried fornicator
Q 5:38 — 'cut off their ___' for theft, 'as recompense for what they committed'
Bukhari — the Prophet's command: 'Whoever changed his Islamic religion, then ___ him'
Q 24:4 — the accuser who fails to produce four witnesses 'shall be flogged with ___ lashes'
Q 24:2 — 'and do not be taken by ___ for them in the religion of Allāh' when applying the lash
Q 4:56 — disbelievers will have their skins replaced in Hell 'so they may taste the ___ again and again'
Q 4:15 — women proven guilty of lewdness: 'confine them to houses until ___, or until Allāh ordains for them [another] way'
Q 5:45 — 'a life for a life, and an eye for an eye, and a nose for a ___'
Q 24:2 — the punishment should be 'witnessed by a ___ of the believers'
Q 5:38 — the hand is cut as 'an exemplary ___ from Allāh'
Q 4:89 — 'take them and kill them wherever you ___' [of hypocrites who turn back]
Q 5:33 — the list of punishments explicitly includes having 'their hands and ___ cut off from opposite sides'

levels.title: Privileges of the Prophet
levels.blurb: Unique allowances granted to Muhammad. Every answer is in the verse.

questions.q: Q 33:50 — uniquely grants the Prophet women 'whom his right hand ___ from what Allāh has returned to you [of captives]'
Q 33:51 — 'You may ___ whom you will of them or take to yourself whom you will' regarding his wives
Q 33:50 — the special allowances are 'only for you, ___ for the [other] believers'
Q 33:21 — the Prophet is described as 'an excellent ___ of conduct'
Q 4:65 — believers do not truly have faith 'until they make you, [O Muhammad], ___ in all disputes that arise between them'
Q 33:56 — 'O you who have believed, ask [Allāh to confer] ___ upon him and salute him with respect'
Q 8:41 — one fifth of all war booty belongs to 'Allāh and for the ___'
Q 33:28 — wives who choose 'this world and its ___' are to be given a gracious release
Bukhari — the Prophet had the strength of ___ men, allowing him to visit all his wives in one night
Q 33:53 — 'it is not lawful for you to ___ the Messenger of Allāh'
Q 33:6 — 'The Prophet is more worthy of the believers than their own ___'
Q 33:37 — Allāh says 'We ___ her to you' about Zaynab, after Zayd divorced her

levels.title: Logical & textual contradictions
levels.blurb: Both sides of each tension are in the text. Find the exact words.

questions.q: Q 2:256 — 'no compulsion in religion'. Q 9:5 commands believers to ___ polytheists unless they repent
Q 4:82 — 'If it had been from other than Allāh, they would have found within it ___ contradiction'
Q 7:54 says creation took six days. Q 41:9-12 lists creation periods that literally sum to ___ days
Q 4:157 — 'they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him; but [another] was made to ___'
Q 96:2 says man was created 'from a clinging clot' — Q 25:54 says from ___
Q 4:171 — describes Jesus as 'a word from Him' and 'a spirit from Him' but warns: 'do not say ___'
Q 38:71 says Adam was created from '___' — Q 96:2 says man was created from 'a clinging clot'
Bukhari — the Prophet 'was bewitched so that he imagined doing things he had not done' — this contradicts the belief that prophets are protected from ___
Q 5:3 — 'This day I have ___ for you your religion' — revealed shortly before the Prophet's death
Q 3:59 — 'the likeness of Jesus with Allāh is as the likeness of ___'
Q 4:43 — 'do not approach prayer while you are ___' — an early partial prohibition before the full ban
Q 3:85 — 'whoever desires other than ___ as religion — never will it be accepted from him'

levels.title: The deep cuts
levels.blurb: Obscure hadiths, lost verses, paradise rewards. The exact words are in the source.

questions.q: Tirmidhi — the Black Stone 'came down from Paradise' and was 'whiter than ___' before sins blackened it
Bukhari (Mi'raj) — daily prayers were originally ___ before Moses negotiated them down to five
Bukhari (Mi'raj) — Muhammad met Ibrahim (Abraham) in the ___ heaven
Ibn Majah — the verse on stoning was written on a paper that 'a tame ___ came in and ate' after the Prophet died
Muslim — Aisha said the final ruling on adult breastfeeding was '___ known sucklings make [marriage] forbidden'
Q 56:35-36 — the companions of Paradise were 'created anew' as 'virgins, ___ of equal age'
Q 78:33 — a Paradise reward is described as 'full-___ [companions] of equal age'
Bukhari (Mi'raj) — in the first heaven Muhammad saw ___ sitting with souls on his right and on his left
Q 17:1 — the night journey took the Prophet 'from al-Masjid al-Haram to al-Masjid al-___'
Muslim — Sahla bint Suhail was told to breastfeed the adult Salim to make him a ___ to her
Q 53:19-20 — 'Then have you considered al-Lat and al-Uzza? And ___, the third — the other one?'
Bukhari (Mi'raj) — each time Muhammad returned to Moses, the daily prayer count was reduced by ___
